title: Character symbolism
wikitext:
[]: Character symbolism?             Quote Post by M. Magdalene » Sat Feb 02, 2008 6:53 am I've looked for this and couldn't find anything. Please forgive me if I missed something. Anyway, I was reading a thread that was analyzing Rei and someone said that you don't really understand her by herself, you understand her when you know what she stands for. And that lead me to think....what does she stand for? I mean, it's probably obvious but has it ever been stated plainly before? And what about Asuka and Shinji? What do they represent? The only theory I've heard that I sort of like is that the three main pilots were symbols for Freud's theory about personality concerning the Id, the Ego and the SuperEgo. But that was about all the detail I could get. Uh, also....is there a place here where new posters can introduce themselves? Because I don't know where that is. Thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Sat Feb 02, 2008 7:08 am Official Welcome Thread I do not understand what you are saying, however, I can say that the entire world is in the 3 pilots' hands, so that symbolises a lot. Rei, in my opinion, was the one that Gendo planned to use to initiate his own version of the Human Instrumentality Project. So in a way, she is vital to Gendo.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Feb 02, 2008 9:02 am IMO, Rei is a walking plot device (with breasts). But there are some archived threads from ANF about Rei: Rei and the Moon Rei, Lilith, and Quantum Mechanics How I Came To Know Rei The Nature of Rei. Again. Allegorical Interpretation (*SPOILERS*) More from here: Rei's Birth Sun & Moon symbolism in Evangelion Explanation on Rei's character and story

Article: Character symbolism
[]: Character symbolism?             Quote Post by M. Magdalene » Sat Feb 02, 2008 6:53 am I've looked for this and couldn't find anything. Please forgive me if I missed something. Anyway, I was reading a thread that was analyzing Rei and someone said that you don't really understand her by herself, you understand her when you know what she stands for. And that lead me to think....what does she stand for? I mean, it's probably obvious but has it ever been stated plainly before? And what about Asuka and Shinji? What do they represent? The only theory I've heard that I sort of like is that the three main pilots were symbols for Freud's theory about personality concerning the Id, the Ego and the SuperEgo. But that was about all the detail I could get. Uh, also....is there a place here where new posters can introduce themselves? Because I don't know where that is. Thoughts?

Rei, in my opinion, was the one that Gendo planned to use to initiate his own version of the Human Instrumentality Project. So in a way, she is vital to Gendo.
